title: Scholarship Application Assistance
transcript: Student: Good morning, Professor Thompson. Thank you for meeting with me. I'm applying for the Johnson Memorial Scholarship, and I was hoping you could give me some feedback on my application essay. Professor: Of course, I'd be happy to help. Let me take a look. Student: Here it is. The prompt asks about my academic goals and how this scholarship would help me achieve them. Professor: This is a strong start. Your introduction is compelling, but I think you could strengthen the second paragraph where you discuss your community service. Can you give a specific example of how your volunteering has shaped your career goals? Student: I've been tutoring at the local community center for two years. I could mention how that experience made me realize I want to work in educational policy. Professor: Exactly. Specific stories are much more memorable than general statements. Also, make sure to explain how the scholarship would reduce your financial burden so you can focus on research. Student: That's a good point. I didn't include that part. Professor: Keep it to one or two sentences. The committee wants to see that you've thought practically about how the funds will be used. Student: Understood. Could you also look at my resume before I submit it? Professor: Sure, send it to me by email, and I'll review it this weekend. The deadline is next Friday, right? Student: Yes, April 14th. I'll send it to you tonight. Thank you so much.

questions.stem: What is the student's main purpose for meeting with Professor Thompson?
options: To request a letter of recommendation
To get feedback on her scholarship application essay
To ask about graduate school options
questions.explanation: The student is applying for a scholarship and wants the professor's feedback on her application essay.

questions.stem: What does the professor suggest the student add to the essay?
options: A list of all her volunteer activities
A description of her high school achievements
An explanation of how the scholarship will reduce her financial burden
Quotes from her teachers about her performance
questions.explanation: The professor advises the student to explain how the scholarship would reduce her financial burden so she can focus on research.

That's a good point. I didn't include that part.
Keep it to one or two sentences. The committee wants to see that you've thought practically about how the funds will be used.
Understood. Could you also look at my resume before I submit it?
